label: Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection

node.label: Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection
node.definition: Best-of-N and verifier-guided selection generate multiple candidates and choose one with an outcome, process, preference, test, or learned quality scorer.
node.why_it_matters: It converts sampling diversity into accuracy gains when the selector is more reliable than any single generation.
limitations: Selection can overfit verifier quirks, consume substantial compute, and discard diverse correct solutions.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection has a `verifies_with` dependency on Self-consistency & best-of-N inference because Self-consistency & best-of-N inference is modeled as evidence or constraints for verification.

In this atlas graph, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection has a `verifies_with` dependency on Outcome verifiers & executable rewards because Outcome verifiers & executable rewards is modeled as evidence or constraints for verification.

In this atlas graph, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection has a `verifies_with` dependency on Process supervision & step-level reward models because Process supervision & step-level reward models is modeled as evidence or constraints for verification.

In this atlas graph, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection has a `uses` dependency on Test-time compute scaling because Test-time compute scaling is modeled as evidence or constraints for verification.

sentence_units.text: Best-of-N and verifier-guided selection generate multiple candidates and choose one with an outcome, process, preference, test, or learned quality scorer.
It converts sampling diversity into accuracy gains when the selector is more reliable than any single generation.
Selection can overfit verifier quirks, consume substantial compute, and discard diverse correct solutions.
